description_long: This greengrocer stall features three large, black, hexagonal umbrellas providing shade over a collection of wooden crates and baskets filled with fresh produce. The stall is set on a cracked concrete surface, giving it an urban, street-market feel. The produce is neatly arranged in rows, with visible items such as apples, oranges, bananas, and possibly eggplants or cucumbers. The crates and baskets are stacked at varying heights, creating a tiered display that maximizes visibility and accessibility. There are no visible signs or price tags, and the structure is open on all sides, allowing easy access for customers. The overall appearance is practical and inviting, typical of a temporary or semi-permanent street market setup.
description: An open-air greengrocer stall with black umbrellas, wooden crates, and baskets displaying colorful fresh produce on a cracked concrete surface.
description_view_0: From this angle, the three black umbrellas and the arrangement of crates and baskets are clearly visible, with some green and yellow produce in the foreground.
description_view_1: This view shows the back of the stall, with the umbrellas dominating the scene and fewer visible produce items.
description_view_2: The side view highlights the depth of the stall, showing the arrangement of crates and a glimpse of red produce, possibly tomatoes or apples.
description_view_3: The front view displays the full array of colorful produce, including oranges, bananas, and green apples, with the umbrellas providing shade overhead.
